name: Mary k. Sobesky's Toffee
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 13x9-inch baking pan and have a cookie sheet and small bowl of ice water ready.
2. Melt butter in a medium-size saucepan.
3. Add sugar and bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
4. Boil for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the candy thermometer reads 300°F to 310°F (hard-crack stage) or when a small amount of syrup forms a brittle mass that snaps easily when pressed between your fingers.
5. Stir in the chopped pecans.
6. Pour into the prepared pan and sprinkle with chocolate chips.
7. Cover the pan with the cookie sheet to help contain the heat so the chips don't melt completely.
8. When the chips are shiny, about 2 minutes later, spread them over the toffee.
9. Chill before cutting into 6 lengthwise slices and 8 slices across.
10. Store airtight in a cool place for up to 2 months.
